Prismatocactus is a genus of cacti in the family Cactaceae, characterized by their cylindrical stems with tubercles and funnel-shaped flowers. The health benefits of Prismatocactus, like many cacti, include anti-inflammatory properties, potential antimicrobial effects, and possible antioxidant activity. The bioactive constituents of Prismatocactus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Prismatocactus include teas, infusions, and salves, which may be used to treat various health issues, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
